howItWorks.intro: This grip strength calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: Percentile Estimation
rules.formula: Based on ratio to age/gender population average
rules.explanation: Your grip strength compared to normative data from population studies.

rules.name: Conversion

howItWorks.outro: Individual results vary based on metabolism, genetics, and activity level. Consult your doctor before starting any program.

faq.q: What is a good grip strength?
faq.a: For adult males, 40-50 kg is average, 50-60 kg is strong, and 60+ kg is excellent. For females, 25-30 kg is average and 35+ kg is excellent. Grip strength peaks around age 25-35 and declines with age.

Grip strength is one of the best predictors of overall health and longevity. Studies show it correlates with cardiovascular health, bone density, and functional independence in older adults. It is also essential for deadlifts, pull-ups, and daily tasks.
